source.description: Six-panel raster marker contact sheet generated for the June 30, 2026 comic chat bubble lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet must show the same bubble, face, heart, color plan, and marker texture through all panels.

process_strategy: The sequence establishes every bubble, face, accent, and color area before the final panel only reinforces marker edges and small highlights.

final_elements.name: rounded chat bubble
final_elements.description: A rounded speech bubble shape with a small tail.

final_elements.name: thick black outline
final_elements.description: A bold marker outline around the bubble and tail.

final_elements.name: cute face
final_elements.description: Two eyes and a small smiling mouth inside the bubble.

final_elements.name: notification badge
final_elements.description: A small circular badge attached to the upper corner.

final_elements.name: notification heart
final_elements.description: A small heart inside the already placed badge.

final_elements.name: drop shadow
final_elements.description: A simple offset shadow below and beside the bubble.

final_elements.name: teal and pink marker fills
final_elements.description: Bright teal bubble fill with pink heart fill and visible marker streaks.

final_elements.name: tiny shine marks
final_elements.description: Small highlights on existing filled shapes.

frames.visible_job: Places the rounded chat bubble and its tail.
Thickens the existing bubble and tail outline with bold marker.
Adds the cute face and small notification heart.
Adds the simple offset shadow under the existing bubble.
Fills the existing bubble and heart with teal and pink marker and adds tiny shine marks.

final_step.visible_job: Retraces existing black outlines, evens marker fills, and clarifies the already placed face, heart, shadow, and shine marks.

rejection_checks: No bubble tail, face, heart, shadow, color area, or highlight should first appear in the final image.
No frame may ink or color a shape that was not already visible earlier.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same bubble pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
